title: Update Workload
content:
# Update Workload

Update Workload

Update the modifiable components of a Workload. The following keys can be
updated individually. If a key is not specified, its existing value will
remain unchanged. If a key is specified, it will completely overwrite the
current value for that key and its nested fields.

- `app_version`
- `cluster_name`
- `node_name`
- `workload_title`
- `runtime.resources`
- `runtime.configuration`
- `system`

## Endpoint

`POST /apps/workloads/{workload_name}/patch`

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.app_workloads.updateWorkload()
print(response)

liveness_probe.title: Liveness Probe
liveness_probe.description: Checks if the container is running. Failing the probe restarts the container.

initial_delay_seconds.title: Initial Delay (s)
initial_delay_seconds.description: Seconds to wait before starting probes.

timeout_seconds.title: Timeout (s)
timeout_seconds.description: Seconds before the probe times out.

http_get.title: HTTP GET Probe
http_get.description: Performs an HTTP GET request to check health.

tcp_socket.title: TCP Socket Probe
tcp_socket.description: Checks if a TCP port is open.

exec.title: Exec Probe
exec.description: Runs a command inside the container to check health.

command.title: Command
command.description: Command to execute. Exit code 0 is healthy.

privileged.description: Run the container in privileged mode.

ports.title: Container Ports
ports.description: List of ports exposed by the container.

exposed.title: Exposed
exposed.description: Expose the port outside the cluster.

volumes.title: Volumes
volumes.description: List of volumes mounted by the container.

target.title: Mount Path
target.description: Path where the volume is mounted.

deployment_type.description:
Specifies the method for deploying a workload on the platform.

- `standard`: The default deployment method. The workload is
automatically created and started.
- `staged+instant-apply`: Similar to `standard`, but if the workload
exists with a different app image, the new image is downloaded before
stopping the existing workload. This minimizes application downtime.
- `staged-only`: Prepares the workload by downloading the necessary
images, but does not create or start the workload. The workload is only
started upon explicit user request (see `/workloads/apply`).

app_type.description:
The type of application.

- `app`: An App that allows mapping inputs and outputs to data
streams, sending control changes, recommendations, and data tags.
- `importer`: Connects to an external system to import data into the
platform as well as receive control changes to act on the external
system.
- `exporter`: Connects to the platform to export data to an external
system.
- `docker`: An external application that does not connect to the
platform's data streams.
- `task`: An application type used internally by the Tasks feature.

download_status.description: Status of downloading Workload to the Edge System.

download_error.description: Simple description of the error in case the image download failed.

status.description: Realtime information about the Workload as seen from the Cloud.

last_seen.description: UTC time when the Workload was last seen by the Cloud, formatted in RFC 3339.

warnings.description: All warnings received for any Workload operations.

type.description: The type of the custom action.
